info.long_name: Diabetes Prevalence from BRFSS Survey
info.short_description: Estimated diabetes prevalence from BRFSS survey data.

info.long_name: BRFSS Chronic Disease Prevalence Estimate
info.short_description: Estimated prevalence of diabetes or obesity from BRFSS telephone survey, by state and year.
info.long_description: Age-stratified state-level prevalence estimates for diabetes and obesity from the CDC Behavioral Risk Factor Surveillance System (BRFSS). Diabetes prevalence is based on self-reported ever-diagnosis; obesity is based on calculated BMI ≥30 from self-reported height and weight. The outcome_name column identifies the condition. Includes 95% confidence intervals (value_lcl, value_ucl) and survey sample size.

info.long_name: Lower 95% Confidence Interval for BRFSS Prevalence
info.short_description: Lower bound of the 95% confidence interval for BRFSS chronic disease prevalence.

info.long_name: Upper 95% Confidence Interval for BRFSS Prevalence
info.short_description: Upper bound of the 95% confidence interval for BRFSS chronic disease prevalence.

info.long_name: BRFSS Survey Sample Size
info.short_description: Number of BRFSS survey respondents used to estimate chronic disease prevalence.

rerun with fixed county data from Epic
fix source naming for epic obesity and diabetes
update formatting on county chronic bundle

info.long_name: Prevalence among Medicare Fee for Service patients of Diabetes
info.short_description: Prevalence among Medicare Fee for Service patients of Diabetes.
info.long_description: Prevalence among Medicare Fee for Service patients of Diabetes. Prevalence rates are calculated by searching for certain diagnosis codes in Medicare beneficiaries’ claims. A beneficiary is considered to have a condition if the CMS administrative data has a claim indicating that the beneficiary is diagnosed for a specific condition over the reference time period (one, two, or three years, depending on the condition chosen) or, in the case of ESRD and disability, if the reason for entitlement (original or current) is listed as disabled or ESRD in the MBSF. The definitions used are the FFS definitions, based on ICD10 codes: https://www2.ccwdata.org/documents/10280/19139421/chr-chronic-condition-algorithms.pdf
info.statement: The viral activity level of Diabetes in wastewater is {value}.

sources.name: Center of Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS)
sources.location: Mapping Medicare Disparities by Population Tool

sources.1.name: Center of Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS)
sources.1.location: Mapping Medicare Disparities by Population Tool

info.long_name: County-Level Chronic Disease Prevalence from Epic and Medicare
info.short_description: Estimated prevalence of diabetes or obesity at county level from Epic Cosmos or Medicare FFS.
info.long_description: County-level prevalence estimates for diabetes and obesity from Epic Cosmos (2025 snapshot) and Medicare Fee-for-Service (CMS, most recent year). For Epic Cosmos: 'Epic Cosmos: HbA1c' uses HbA1c ≥6.5% for diabetes; 'Epic Cosmos: BMI' uses measured BMI ≥30 for obesity. For Medicare FFS: diagnosis based on CCW ICD-10 algorithm. The source column identifies the data source and clinical definition.

info.long_name: Percentage of 2021 County Population Captured in Epic Cosmos
info.short_description: Percentage of the 2021 county population represented in the Epic Cosmos patient panel, by age group.

info.long_name: Number of Patients in County-Level Analysis
info.short_description: Number of patients used in the county-level analysis. Small counts from Epic Cosmos are reported as '10 or fewer'.

info.long_name: Epic Cosmos State-Level Chronic Disease Prevalence
info.short_description: Estimated prevalence of diabetes or obesity from Epic Cosmos EHR, by state and year.
info.long_description: State-level prevalence estimates for diabetes and obesity from Epic Cosmos electronic health records. The source column identifies the clinical definition: 'Epic Cosmos: HbA1c' uses HbA1c ≥6.5% for diabetes; 'Epic Cosmos: ICD10' uses CCW ICD-10 diagnosis codes for both conditions; 'Epic Cosmos: BMI' uses measured BMI ≥30 for obesity. Values represent the percentage of active Epic patients meeting the specified criterion.

info.long_name: Percentage of 2021 State Population Captured in Epic Cosmos
info.short_description: Percentage of the 2021 state population represented in the Epic Cosmos patient panel, by age group.
info.long_description: Estimated percentage of the 2021 Census population in each age group that is represented in the Epic Cosmos patient panel for a given state. Used as an indicator of Epic's geographic coverage and potential selection bias. Lower values indicate that Epic data covers a smaller fraction of the state's population.

info.long_name: Number of Epic Cosmos Patients in State-Level Analysis
info.short_description: Number of Epic Cosmos patients in the state-level analysis. Values ≤10 are reported as '10 or fewer'.

Diabetes.short_description: Estimated prevalence of diabetes among adults.
Diabetes.long_description: Estimated prevalence of diabetes among adults, from BRFSS (self-reported diagnosis), Epic Cosmos (HbA1c ≥6.5% or ICD-10 diagnosis codes), and Medicare FFS (CCW algorithm). The source column identifies the data source and clinical definition used.

Obesity.short_description: Estimated prevalence of obesity (BMI ≥30) among adults.
Obesity.long_description: Estimated prevalence of obesity among adults, from BRFSS (self-reported height/weight, BMI ≥30), Epic Cosmos (measured BMI ≥30 or ICD-10 diagnosis codes), and Medicare FFS (CCW algorithm). The source column identifies the data source and clinical definition used.

info.long_name: Diabetes Prevalence from BRFSS Survey
info.short_description: Estimated diabetes prevalence from BRFSS survey data.

info.long_name: Prevalence among Medicare Fee for Service patients of Diabetes
info.short_description: Prevalence among Medicare Fee for Service patients of Diabetes.
info.long_description: Prevalence among Medicare Fee for Service patients of Diabetes. Prevalence rates are calculated by searching for certain diagnosis codes in Medicare beneficiaries’ claims. A beneficiary is considered to have a condition if the CMS administrative data has a claim indicating that the beneficiary is diagnosed for a specific condition over the reference time period (one, two, or three years, depending on the condition chosen) or, in the case of ESRD and disability, if the reason for entitlement (original or current) is listed as disabled or ESRD in the MBSF. The definitions used are the FFS definitions, based on ICD10 codes: https://www2.ccwdata.org/documents/10280/19139421/chr-chronic-condition-algorithms.pdf
info.statement: The viral activity level of Diabetes in wastewater is {value}.

sources.name: Center of Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS)
sources.location: Mapping Medicare Disparities by Population Tool

sources.1.name: Center of Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS)
sources.1.location: Mapping Medicare Disparities by Population Tool

info.long_name: Chronic Disease Prevalence by Geography, Year, and Source
info.short_description: Estimated prevalence of diabetes or obesity by state and year from multiple sources.
info.long_description: Combined state-level prevalence estimates for diabetes and obesity from CDC BRFSS (telephone survey), Epic Cosmos EHR (HbA1c, ICD-10, and BMI-based definitions), and Medicare Fee-for-Service administrative claims. The outcome_name column identifies the condition (Diabetes or Obesity); the source column identifies the data source and clinical definition. These sources are not directly comparable due to differences in population covered (all adults vs. Epic patients vs. Medicare beneficiaries ≥65), case definitions, and ascertainment methodology.

info.long_name: Sample Size for Prevalence Estimate
info.short_description: Survey respondents (BRFSS), patient count (Epic), or beneficiary count (Medicare) for the prevalence estimate.
info.long_description: Sample size underlying the prevalence estimate. Interpretation varies by source: for BRFSS, this is the number of survey respondents; for Epic Cosmos, this is the number of active patients (values ≤10 are reported as '10 or fewer'); for Medicare FFS, this is the number of beneficiaries with a Medicare claim.

bundle_chronic_diseases/dist/epic_preval.short_description: Number of patients used in the county-level analysis. Small counts from Epic Cosmos are reported as '10 or fewer'.

bundle_chronic_diseases/dist/prevalence_.long_name: Chronic Disease Prevalence by Geography, Year, and Source
bundle_chronic_diseases/dist/prevalence_.short_description: Estimated prevalence of diabetes or obesity by state and year from multiple sources.
bundle_chronic_diseases/dist/prevalence_.long_description: Combined state-level prevalence estimates for diabetes and obesity from CDC BRFSS (telephone survey), Epic Cosmos EHR (HbA1c, ICD-10, and BMI-based definitions), and Medicare Fee-for-Service administrative claims. The outcome_name column identifies the condition (Diabetes or Obesity); the source column identifies the data source and clinical definition. These sources are not directly comparable due to differences in population covered (all adults vs. Epic patients vs. Medicare beneficiaries ≥65), case definitions, and ascertainment methodology.

bundle_chronic_diseases/dist/prevalence_.short_description: Survey respondents (BRFSS), patient count (Epic), or beneficiary count (Medicare) for the prevalence estimate.
bundle_chronic_diseases/dist/prevalence_.long_description: Sample size underlying the prevalence estimate. Interpretation varies by source: for BRFSS, this is the number of survey respondents; for Epic Cosmos, this is the number of active patients (values ≤10 are reported as '10 or fewer'); for Medicare FFS, this is the number of beneficiaries with a Medicare claim.
